title: Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost
content:
{{Top|rwm|new|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the comic book miniseries|[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost (trade paperback)|the trade paperback]], [[Crimson Empire III|the in-universe holofilm]] or the single issue [[Crimson Empire 3]]}} {{ComicSeries |image=[[File:CrimsonEmpireIII-EmpireLost-TPB.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' |writer=*[[Mike Richardson]] *[[Randy Stradley]] |penciller=[[Paul Gulacy]] |inker= |letterer=[[Michael Heisler]] |colorist=[[Michael Bartolo]] |cover artist=[[Dave Dorman]] |editor= |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |start date=[[October 26]], [[2011]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54051/star_wars_crimson_empire_iii_-_empire_lost_2011_1|text=Star Wars: Crimson Empire III - Empire Lost (2011) #1}} |end date=[[April 25]], [[2012]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54056/star_wars_crimson_empire_iii_-_empire_lost_2011_6|text=Star Wars: Crimson Empire III - Empire Lost (2011) #6}} |schedule= |format= |issues=6 |timeline=[[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]]{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/crimsonempire3/index.html|text=Back in the Red: Dark Horse Comics Announces Long-Awaited Crimson Empire III Series}} places ''Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' to two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood]]'', which ''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' must take place in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]]. |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost''''' is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] miniseries and the sequel to ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood]]'', set in the wake of the [[Battle of Nam Chorios]], and the finale of [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire (series)|the trilogy]]. Like its predecessors, it is a six-part miniseries co-written by [[Mike Richardson]] and [[Randy Stradley]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. Though originally canceled, the series was revived for a [[2011]]–[[2012]] release. About the time that the first issue was released, [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] created a caption contest for two people to win a copy of the first issue signed by three of the creators. ==Plot summary== Two years after the events of ''Council of Blood'', Kir Kanos is sought by a man named Ennix Devian, who is a threat to the galaxy large enough for [[Vima-Da-Boda]] to warn Jedi Master Luke Skywalker of Devian, the leader of a splinter faction of the Imperial Remnant known as the Restored Empire, wishes to reform the Empire under the ideals of Palpatine in the same vein as Kanos. To this end, Devian hires bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to retrieve Kanos, who was operating under his "Kenix Kil" pseudonym in the backwater region of Meenka, and bring him to the Restored Empire homebase of RZ7-6113-23. There, Kanos is acquainted with a massive army and fleet made up of Clone Wars-era hardware, which Devian plans to use to attack the homeworlds of both the Imperial Remnant and the New Republic and create a new order. Meanwhile, political turmoil erupts on various worlds due to the influence of Nom Anor, a man of unknown origin who arranges the theft of a small supply of the volatile and highly explosive material zinethium from a storehouse on Nyara. Recognizing Devian as a corrupt "thug" and assassin with no moral value, Kanos rejects his political ideals, escaping from the base in a captured shuttle. He travels to Coruscant in an attempt to make amends with Mirith Sinn and warn her of Devian's imminent attack; however, he is unaware that the shuttle has been embedded with the zinethium stolen from Nyara, which is abandoned in orbit by Luke Skywalker just as it detonates. The blame falls on Kanos, who was accompanying Sinn to a possible peace treaty between the Imperial Remnant and the New Republic hosted by the D'Asta family. Soon after the attempted attack on Coruscant, the peace summit is disrupted by a squad of Devian's stormtroopers, and Feena D'Asta is killed in the ensuing firefight. With peace talks halted by what seemed to be a New Republic ambush, Admiral Gilad Pellaeon of the Imperial Remnant quickly launches a retaliatory fleet towards Coruscant; with the Imperial homeworld of Orinda left relatively undefended, Devian mobilizes his fleet to attack. Amidst this political and military confusion, Kanos and Sinn make an alliance with Feena's father, Ragez, who offers his fleet to ambush Devian's fleet as it enters the Orinda system. They narrowly save Admiral Pellaeon from capture by Devian during the battle, and Kanos challenges Devian to a final duel. Kanos overcomes Devian and kills him, but is critically wounded in the process. He uses this opportunity to fake his death, allowing the New Republic to pardon him "posthumously" of his crimes while he recovered from his wounds in one of Ragez D'Asta's safehouses. D'Asta and his remaining daughter, the clone of Feena created by Black Sun which he adopted, help Kanos begin a new life, one without the Empire. ==Development== {{Quote|I patterned Crimson Empire after the Japanese tale of the forty-seven Ronin - not in the story itself, but in the quest for revenge. How I see it in the first Crimson Empire series is that it deals with laying out the ground work and its interaction with the initial characters, with actually somewhat of a tragic ending to the first series. I see it as a trilogy: the second part being the quest for Luke Skywalker - for his part in the death of the Emperor; the third part, the revenge on the ruling council. It'll be a story-cycle that takes them through personal discovery, great adventure, and intrigue in the Empire itself. And ultimately the opportunity for revenge, where Kir Kanos finds himself.|[[Mike Richardson]]|{{InsiderCite|36|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth}}}} ''Crimson Empire III'' was originally planned for release in [[2001]] and later [[2002]], but the project was eventually shelved. The basic plot, set in the [[New Jedi Order era]],{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/novel/news20010723.html|text=Comic-Con Book Report: Publishing Horizon}} was to have [[Kir Kanos]] fight [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the last one left who was complicit in [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] death. The concept of an [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guard]] fighting Skywalker was a concept Stradley developed for [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel's ''Star Wars'' series]], but was not allowed to use; it was the origin of the original ''Crimson Empire'' concept. In [[2008]], Mike Richardson announced that he and Stradley were working on the project again to finally wrap up the ''Crimson Empire'' saga.{{WebCite|url=http://www.comicbookresources.com/?page=article&id=17888|text=Comic Book Resources > CBR TV: Mike Richardson|archivedate=20080829105609}} Several days later, however, Stradley announced that although there had been talks, the project was not going to happen.{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=Help/Boards/viewtopic.php?t=14275&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=15|text=Dark Horse Comics :: Boards :: Crimson Empire news! (with video proof from CBR!)|nobackup=1}} In [[2010]], at [[Celebration V]], ''Crimson Empire III'' was announced for a [[2011]] release. Richardson and Stradley would be writing, with [[Paul Gulacy]] on the art.{{WebCite|url=http://www.newsarama.com/comics/star-wars-celebration-v-dark-horse-100812.html|text=Newsarama.com : STAR WARS Celebration V: DARK HORSE COMICS LIVE|archivedate=20100815221246}} A prequel story titled ''[[The Third Time Pays for All]]'' was published in ''Dark Horse Presents #1'' in [[April]] 2011. In [[July]] [[2011]], the solicit for the first issue was released, revealing the miniseries' subtitle as ''Empire Lost''.{{WebCite|url=http://www.newsarama.com/comics/dark-horse-october-2011-solicitations-110708.html|text=Newsarama.com : DARK HORSE OCTOBER 2011 Solicitations: STAR WARS, MASS EFFECT|archivedate=20110711132941}} ==Continuity== The opening line of issue one places this ten years after the death of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], which would logically place ''Empire Lost'' in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost}} ===Issues=== {| {{Prettytable}} ! colspan="3" |Color code key: |- |style="background-color:#FFBB60;" align="center" width="33%" |Collected (TPB) issue |style="background-color:#40CC40;" align="center" width="33%" |Released issue |style="background-color:#FFFF80;" align="center" width="33%" |Future issue |} {|{{Prettytable}} !
